description:
The Jaffe residence at 1608 Wilderness Gate is being outfitted with an advanced integration of home systems, ensuring seamless control and enhanced living experiences. The integration is centered around the Control4 platform, which will manage whole-house audio, two distinct home theater configurations, and various other smart home features.

Audio and Home Theater:
Whole-House Audio: The residence will feature a Sonos-based music system with two zones of Sonos ports, providing high-quality audio throughout the home.
Home Theater Configurations:
Living Room Theater: Equipped with an Integra surround receiver connected to Sonance invisible speakers, delivering an immersive surround sound experience without visible hardware.
Master Bedroom Theater: A 3.1 system featuring an Episode soundbar paired with a petite surround system from Integra. The setup includes an in-wall subwoofer from Episode, ensuring deep bass and a rich audio experience.
Network and Power Management:
Network: The wireless network will be meshed to provide strong and consistent connectivity across the property. The network will be hardwired with Arraknis networking equipment, ensuring reliable and high-speed internet access.
Power Management: A Wattbox UPS will be integrated into the network, providing power protection and management for all connected systems.
Security and Monitoring:
Security System: The residence will be secured with a Honeywell Security Vista System, featuring door contacts and smoke/carbon monoxide detectors. The system will be monitored by CMS (Criticom Monitoring Services), ensuring 24/7 professional security monitoring.
Climate Control:
Thermostats: Control4 thermostats will manage both the air conditioning system and multiple radiant heat systems, allowing for precise climate control throughout the home.
Additional Systems:
Shade Pre-Wiring: Sound Look will be responsible for the pre-wiring of shades, with final installations to be handled by others.
Irrigation: A Rachio irrigation system will be deployed, with the specific location yet to be determined, ensuring efficient water management for the landscaping.
This comprehensive setup ensures that all systems work together harmoniously, providing the Jaffe residence with cutting-edge technology and convenience in everyday living.

description:
•Remove existing roofing materials down to the deck

•Mechanically fasten or fully adhere a 60 mil TPO roofing system per special
instructions, installed over ISO, fanfold, or EPS insulation to achieve adequate slope.
•Flash all penetrations and curbs, including installing canales with TPO-coated canale
liner as required.
• Terminate perimeter with termination bar, approximate to the height permitted by
existing stucco stop, fully wrapped where applicable.
(Note: Regret flashing is not included.)
• Install 3’x3’ metal edging where applicable.
• Clean up and haul away all debris.
• Submit the necessary permit application for the project.

description:
Specifications: To existing roof of house, install Mule-Hide 60 MIL Single-Ply T.P.O. Roof System to existing Modified Bitumen / roof system at approximately 1,664 square feet, including vertical flashing; perform the following: 

•Hold a pre-construction meeting to discuss the project with homeowner & project manager.

•Apply for building permit, post on site and coordinate inspection.

•Coordinate with the client's HVAC technician to ensure HVAC units are properly disconnected & removed from the roof field.

•Cover walkways, landscaping, sculptures, etc. that need protection. Mobilize port-a-potty and dumpster.

•Tear off and dispose of one layers of existing roof.

•Inspect roof deck for deteriorated material. As required, all defective decking material will be replaced at $6.00/square foot under a separate Change Request.

•As required, repair or replace deteriorated vents with new two-way industry standard vents every 400 square feet per code. 

•Install additional counter-slope "cricketing" at lower corners and between canales to minimize surface accumulation of moisture.

•Install Coverboard over entire existing deck. 

•Install approximately 5 linear feet of “Z-Bar” surface reglet flashing to roof/stucco interface banked HVAC trunk, per code requirements. Touch up stucco base & color coat as needed around new flashing. 

•Apply bonding adhesive to entire roof. Install new Mule-Hide 60 MIL Single-Ply T.P.O. Roof System. 

•Re-line all canales/scuppers. 

•Seal all roof penetrations using heatweld method (fishmouth procedure) to ensure longevity of system– pipes, vents, ducts, flues, etc.

•Affix reinforced vertical TPO wall flashing to internal facing parapet structure.

•Clean and dispose of construction debris.

•Conduct final walkthrough to confirm homeowner's expectations are met.

description:
Re-Roof Entire Residence 105 The High Rd

Remove all Gravel, Tear off Existing Roof Membrane down to original deck slope in deck.
Remove all Canales, Open Top of Parapet Walls.

Install New Standard Wood Canales Lined in 26 Gauge Metal with TPO Coating Build up all Corners and in between Canales (Crickets) to improve Drainage and avoid ponding issues with Rigid Insulation.

Install 1" Rigid Insulation on entire field of Roof Mechanically Fastened with Screws and Plates.

Install 60 Mil (Rock Brown) TPO Membrane Fully Adhered with Substrate Adhesive.

Flash all Parapet Walls, Skylight Curbs. Remove and replace all Roof Breather Vents (8) Caulk/Glue and Heat Weld all Flashings, Roof Penetrations, Canales and Seams on Field of Roof to Manufacturer Specifications.

Remove and replace all Skylights Dome (5) 
(3) 27"x51" White Frost (2) 26 1/2"x 26 1/2" White Frost.

description:
Install a new 200A/200A OH ringless meter/main w/ feed through lugs in the existing meter/main location. Install a new mast and weatherhead so that it extends at least 3’ above the surface of the roof. Install a new grounding electrode system with two ground rods at least 6’ apart to comply with NEC 250.53(B) with #6 bare CU wire. Install a surge protection device to comply with NEC article 230.67. An 811 will be required to drive the new ground rods. Install a 50A/2P breaker and connect the existing trenched sub panel feeder wire. Run new #2/0 SER cable from the feed through lugs in the meter/main to the existing 150A interior panel. Install a new 150A main breaker in the interior load center. Ensure grounds and neutrals are separated in the interior panel and install a ground bar if necessary to isolate them.

Interconnect the PV system with a 35A/2P breaker in the new 200A/200A meter main.
